title: Apparatus and methods for surgical lighting
abstract: An apparatus for surgical lighting is disclosed. The apparatus includes a light emitting element formed with a light source encapsulated within an outer layer. The outer layer includes a biocompatible material. A power supply is coupled to the light source. The apparatus includes an actuating mechanism that controls power from the power supply to the light source to emit light along a surgical area. The light emitting element provides enhanced illumination and other surgical advantages.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1 . An apparatus for surgical lighting, comprising: an outer layer, the outer layer being biocompatible; a substrate enclosed within the outer layer; a light source mounted along the substrate; a power supply coupled to the light source; and an actuating mechanism configured to activate the power supply for emitting light from the light source. 2. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the outer layer comprises polydimethylsiloxane. 3. The apparatus of claim 1 , further comprising: an additional layer oriented around the outer layer, the additional layer including a sponge material to reduce engagement to bodily tissue such that the apparatus is removable from a surgical area. 4. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the outer layer is malleable and memory- retaining, and wherein the substrate includes a flexible printed circuit board. 5. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the power supply includes a disposable battery enclosed within the outer layer. 6. The apparatus of claim 1 , further comprising an insulated electrical conduit coupled between the light source and the power supply. 7. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the light source, power supply, actuating mechanism, and the outer layer collectively form a discrete light emitting element oriented along a surgical area. 8. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the light source emits the light at a predefined wavelength corresponding to a predefined surgical function. 9. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the light source includes a plurality of light emitting diodes encapsulated within the outer layer. 10. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the plurality of light emitting diodes are oriented as a columnar array within the outer layer. 1 1 . The apparatus of claim 9, wherein a first one of the plurality of light emitting diodes emits the light at a first wavelength, and a second one of the plurality of light emitting diodes emits the light at a second wavelength. 12. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the plurality of light emitting diodes are oriented in a predetermined arrangement within the outer layer to provide focused illumination along a surgical area. 13. The apparatus of claim 1 , further comprising a plurality of radio-opaque markers formed along the outer layer or the light source. 14. The apparatus of claim 1 , further comprising a retrieval tether coupled to the outer layer configured for removing the apparatus from a surgical area. 15. The apparatus of claim 1 , further comprising: an antennae coupled to the power supply, the actuating mechanism configured to transmit a radio-frequency signal to the antennae to activate the power supply and emit the light from the light source. 16. The apparatus of claim 1 , further comprising a reed switch for engaging the power supply, the reed switch configured to activate the power supply in response to a magnet to emit the light from the light source. 17. An apparatus for surgical lighting, comprising: 

 an outer layer, the outer layer being biocompatible and insulating; a light source enclosed within the outer layer; and a power supply coupled to the light source. 18. The apparatus of claim 17, wherein the light source comprises a light emitting diode tape, the light emitting diode tape being malleable and memory- retaining. 19. A method of forming an apparatus for surgical lighting, comprising: providing a light source, the light source configured to illuminate a surgical area; encapsulating the light source within an outer layer, the outer layer being biocompatible; and coupling the light source to a power supply. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the power supply is encapsulated within the outer layer and positioned adjacent the light source.
